name: node-v8-compile-cache
title: node-v8-compile-cache
summary: Leverage v8 compile cache to speedup loading of Node.js modules
description:
Leverage v8 compile cache to speedup loading of Node.js modules
V8 is the JavaScript Engine used by Node.js and Chromium; it uses just-in-time
compilation (JIT) to execute Javascript code, and code caching to lessen the
overhead to initially parse and compile a script.
.
The ability to tap into V8 to produce/consume this cache was introduced in
Node v5.7.0. This module attaches a require hook to use V8's code cache to
speed up instantiation time.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-v8-compile-cache because Leverage v8 compile cache to speedup loading of Node.js modules.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-v8-compile-cache as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-v8-compile-cache with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-v8-compile-cache --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
